{tex}7+8 \times 9{/tex}
{tex}=7+72{/tex}

questions.question: In the given figure, PQ and RS are parallel lines. If {tex}\angle 1=(2 {a}+{b})^{\circ}{/tex} and {tex}\angle 6=(3 {a}-{b})^{\circ}{/tex}, then the measure of {tex}\angle 2{/tex} in terms of b is

Given:

{tex}\angle 1=(2 a+b)^{\circ}{/tex}
{tex}\angle 6=(3 a-b)^{\circ}{/tex}
Since {tex}P Q \| R S, \angle 1{/tex} and {tex}\angle 6{/tex} are corresponding angles.
Therefore,

{tex}2 a+b =3 a-b{/tex}
{tex}a =2 b{/tex}
From the figure, {tex}\angle 1{/tex} and {tex}\angle 2{/tex} form a linear pair.

{tex} \angle 1+\angle 2=180^{\circ}{/tex}
Substituting the value of {tex}\angle 1{/tex} from the figure and simplifying using {tex}a=2 b{/tex}, we get

{tex} \angle 2=(108-b)^{\circ}{/tex}

Step 1:

Step 2:
Using the properties of parallel lines, angle {tex}a{/tex} is equal to the given corresponding angle.

{tex} a=50^{\circ}{/tex}
Step 3:
Angles {tex}a{/tex} and {tex}b{/tex} form a linear pair.

{tex}a+b=180^{\circ}{/tex}

Step 2:

Step 3:

So, {tex}\angle Q P R=130^{\circ}-50^{\circ}=80^{\circ}{/tex}

questions.question: In the given figure, lines I and m intersect each other at a point. Which of the following is false?
questions.explanation:
When two lines intersect, vertically opposite angles are equal, and adjacent angles form a linear pair (sum = {tex}180^{\circ}{/tex}).

So:

  • {tex}\angle a=\angle b{/tex} (vertically opposite angles)
  • {tex}\angle c=\angle d{/tex} (vertically opposite angles)
  • {tex}\angle a+\angle d=180^{\circ}{/tex} (linear pair)
  • {tex}\angle a=\angle d{/tex} (not true in general)

  • {tex}\angle 2{/tex} and {tex}\angle 6{/tex}
  • {tex}\angle 3{/tex} and {tex}\angle 7{/tex}
  • {tex}\angle 4{/tex} and {tex}\angle 8{/tex}

Total pairs {tex}=4{/tex}

questions.question: In parallelogram {tex}A B C D, A B{/tex} is parallel to {tex}D C{/tex}, and {tex}A D{/tex} is parallel to {tex}B C{/tex}. {tex}A B{/tex} is produced to {tex}E{/tex}, such that {tex}\angle C B E=55^{\circ}{/tex}. {tex}B C{/tex} is produced to {tex}F{/tex}, such that {tex}B C F{/tex} is a straight line. The values of {tex}x, y{/tex}, and {tex}z{/tex}, respectively, are
questions.explanation:
Given:

  • In parallelogram {tex}A B C D, A B \| D C{/tex} and {tex}A D \| B C{/tex}
  • {tex}A B{/tex} is extended to {tex}E{/tex} and {tex}\angle C B E=55^{\circ}{/tex}
  • {tex}B C{/tex} is extended to {tex}F{/tex}, so {tex}B C F{/tex} is a straight line

Step 1:
Since {tex}B C E{/tex} forms a straight line, linear pair gives:

{tex} \angle A B C=180^{\circ}-55^{\circ}=125^{\circ}{/tex}

Step 2:
In a parallelogram, opposite angles are equal and adjacent angles are supplementary, so corresponding angles formed by parallel lines give required values.

Step 3:
Using parallel line properties, remaining angles become:

  • {tex}x=55^{\circ}{/tex}
  • {tex}y=125^{\circ}{/tex}
  • {tex}z=125^{\circ}{/tex}

questions.question: The number of all rhythms of short and long syllables having 9 beats is
questions.explanation:
The number of rhythms of short and long syllables having {tex}n{/tex} beats follows the Virahanka-Fibonacci sequence:

{tex} 1,2,3,5,8,13,21,34,55, \ldots{/tex}
Counting the terms:

  • 1 beat {tex}\rightarrow 1{/tex}
  • 2 beats {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} 2
  • 3 beats {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} 3
  • 4 beats {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} 5
  • 5 beats {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} 8
  • 6 beats {tex}\rightarrow 13{/tex}
  • 7 beats {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} 21
  • 8 beats {tex}\rightarrow 34{/tex}
  • 9 beats {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} 55

Therefore, the number of all rhythms having 9 beats is 55.

  • 3rd term = 3
  • 4th term = 5
  • 5th term {tex}=8{/tex}
  • 6th term {tex}=13{/tex}
  • 7th term = 21

Therefore, the 7th term is 21.

  • 3rd even number {tex}=6=2 \times 3{/tex}

Hence, the {tex}n{/tex}th even number is:

{tex} 2 n{/tex}

questions.question: The greatest number which always divides the product of the predecessor and successor of an odd natural number other than 1, is ________.

Let the odd natural number be n ({tex}{n}>1{/tex}).
Since {tex}n{/tex} is odd, its predecessor is ({tex}n-1{/tex}) which is even, and its successor is ({tex}n+1{/tex}) which is also even.
So, {tex}(n-1)(n+1)=n^2-1{/tex}.
For any odd number {tex}{n}, {n}^2-1{/tex} is always divisible by 8.
Therefore, the greatest number that always divides the product is 8.

questions.question: Divide 0.84 m of cloth into pieces of 0.04m each. How many pieces will you get?
questions.explanation:
Length of cloth {tex}=0.84 {~m}{/tex}
Length of each piece {tex}=0.04 {~m}{/tex}
Number of pieces:

{tex} 0.84 \div 0.04{/tex}
Remove the decimal points by multiplying both numbers by 100 :

{tex}\frac{0.84 \times 100}{0.04 \times 100}=\frac{84}{4}{/tex}
{tex}84 \div 4=21{/tex}
Therefore, the number of pieces is:

21

questions.question: A bottle contains 0.85L of juice. How many bottles of the same size can be filled from 17L of juice?
questions.explanation:
Total juice {tex}=17 {~L}{/tex}
Capacity of one bottle {tex}=0.85 {~L}{/tex}
Number of bottles:

{tex} 17 \div 0.85{/tex}
Remove the decimal point from the divisor by multiplying both numbers by 100:

{tex}\frac{17 \times 100}{0.85 \times 100}=\frac{1700}{85}{/tex}
{tex}1700 \div 85=20{/tex}
Therefore, 20 bottles can be filled.

questions.question: A small box weighs 0.36 kg. If 0.45 such boxes are used for packing, what is the combined weight?
questions.explanation:
Weight of one box {tex}=0.36 {~kg}{/tex}
Number of boxes {tex}=0.45{/tex}
Combined weight:

{tex} 0.36 \times 0.45{/tex}
Multiply as whole numbers:

{tex} 36 \times 45=1620{/tex}
There are {tex}2+2=4{/tex} decimal places in the factors.
So, place the decimal point four places from the right:

{tex}0.1620{/tex}
{tex}=0.162{/tex}
Therefore, the combined weight is:

{tex} 0.162 {~kg}{/tex}

questions.question: Divide 8.64 m of rope into pieces of 0.12 m each. How many pieces will there be?
questions.explanation:
Length of rope {tex}=8.64 {~m}{/tex}
Length of each piece {tex}=0.12 {~m}{/tex}
Number of pieces:

{tex} 8.64 \div 0.12{/tex}
Remove the decimal points by multiplying both numbers by 100 :

{tex} \frac{8.64 \times 100}{0.12 \times 100}=\frac{864}{12}{/tex}
Now divide:

{tex} 864 \div 12=72{/tex}
Therefore, the number of pieces is:

72

questions.question: A car travels 18.9 km in 0.3 hours. What is its speed in km/h?
questions.explanation:
Distance travelled = 18.9 km
Time taken {tex}=0.3 {~h}{/tex}
Speed:

{tex}\text { Speed }=\frac{\text { Distance }}{\text { Time }}{/tex}
{tex}=18.9 \div 0.3{/tex}
Remove the decimal points by multiplying both numbers by 10 :

{tex}\frac{18.9 \times 10}{0.3 \times 10}=\frac{189}{3}{/tex}
{tex}189 \div 3=63{/tex}
Therefore,

{tex} \text { Speed }=63 {~km} / {h}{/tex}

questions.question: A juice factory has 9.6 L of juice. It is poured equally into bottles of 0.8 L each. How many bottles can be filled completely?
questions.explanation:
Total juice {tex}=9.6 {~L}{/tex}
Capacity of each bottle {tex}=0.8 {~L}{/tex}
Number of bottles:

{tex} 9.6 \div 0.8{/tex}

Remove the decimal points by multiplying both numbers by 10 :

{tex}\frac{9.6 \times 10}{0.8 \times 10}=\frac{96}{8}{/tex}
{tex}96 \div 8=12{/tex}
Therefore, 12 bottles can be filled completely.

questions.question: The cost of 1 kg of flour is ₹ 52. If Meera buys 3.5 kg of flour, how much does she pay?

questions.question: A grocer has 14.2 kg of sugar. He wants to pack it into bags weighing 0.25 kg each. How many bags can he fill completely?

Number of bags:

{tex} 14.2 \div 0.25{/tex}

{tex} 0.25=\frac{1}{4}{/tex}
Dividing by {tex}\frac{1}{4}{/tex} is the same as multiplying by 4:

{tex}14.2 \div 0.25=14.2 \times 4{/tex}
{tex}=56.8{/tex}
Therefore, the number of bags that can be filled is:

{tex} 56.8{/tex}

questions.question: The product of 8.12 and 7.25 is:
questions.explanation:

{tex} 2+2=4{/tex}

{tex}58.8700=58.870{/tex}

questions.question: If 9.875 is divided by 1.75, what is the quotient?

questions.question: If {tex}64 \times 5=320{/tex}, then {tex}0.64 \times 0.5{/tex} is equal to
questions.explanation:

{tex}=0.32{/tex}
